title: Cache document pages
description: When the text is extracted from a document, it should be stored in a file at .northrop/cache/text/{hash}.md, where the {hash} is the original document file hash. The page character indexes should be stored in a {hash}.json file. This cache should be checked before text is extracted for any document to avoid multiple re-extractions on the same document.

title: Use Markitdown port
description: Add a new file handler that uses the Markitdown port at https://github.com/managedcode/markitdown instead of MuPDF (which is how the current handler works). Add this as an additional alternative to the MuPDF port, with '--handler markitdown' option added to the 'extract' command in the 'Common.AI.Demo' project.

title: Add FTS5 search handler to the DocumentSearchService
description: Add SQLite FTS5 extension support to the DocumentLibrary and DocumentSearchService to allow full text search of the text content stored in the DocumentLibrary. This is not natively supported by EF Core so it will need to be added as raw SQL queries unless you can find evidence to the contrary online.

title: Library not initialized when project is opened
description: When opening a project, the document library should be initialized by ProjectService.OpenProjectAsync, but this appears to be failing silently. Tools like Library and Parrot then fail because the library is not ready. The Library tool shows 'Document library not initialised' and the Parrot tool shows 'Failed to initialize document library for project'. This is a regression from the fix in commit 699e01d3.

title: Modernise CI/CD workflow
description:
Comprehensive modernisation of the CI/CD system, migrating from PowerShell + napman to Cake Build (C# Make).

KEY CHANGES FROM CURRENT SYSTEM

Deployment Trigger: Workflow Dispatch (not labeled PRs)
Replace label-based deployment with manual workflow dispatch for explicit control.

Benefits:
- Version calculated before tag creation (not after)
- GitHub Release created with correct tag from the start
- Two clicks to deploy - simple and explicit
- Aligns with GitHub intended release workflow

Auto-Label PRs by Path
Automatically label PRs based on changed files using .github/labeler.yml
- app:Octopus for src/Applications.Octopus and src/Common.UI
- app:Taipan for src/Applications.Taipan and src/Common.Revit
- app:Retriever for src/Applications.Retriever

Filtered Release Notes
Generate app-specific release notes by querying PRs with the app label merged since last release.

GitHub Releases
Create proper GitHub Releases with auto-generated CalVer tag, filtered release notes, and link to workflow run.

---

CAKE MIGRATION

Why Cake Frosting?
- Cross-platform build automation with C# DSL
- Strong typing and IDE support
- Unit testable build tasks
- Cake v6.0.0 supports .NET 8/9/10
- Reuse existing Common.Applications logic

Phase 1: Foundation
- Create Cake Frosting project (src/Build/)
- Create BuildContext with shared settings
- Port CalVer logic (reuse Common.Applications.AppVersion)
- Port manifest logic (reuse Common.Applications.AppManifest)
- Create externalised config (build-config.json)

Phase 2: Core Tasks
- UpdateVersionTask (replaces napman update)
- BuildTask (replaces build.ps1)
- PackageTask (replaces package.ps1)
- UploadTask (replaces upload.ps1 + proper error handling)
- CollectManifestsTask (replaces napman collect)
- DeployTask (orchestrates all above)
- CreateReleaseTask (creates GitHub Release with filtered notes)

Phase 3: Application-Specific Tasks
- VelopackPackageTask for Retriever
- MultiVersionBuildTask for Taipan (Revit 2023-2026)
- MultiVersionPackageTask for Taipan

Phase 4: CI/CD Integration
- Update deploy.yml to use workflow_dispatch + Cake
- Add .github/labeler.yml for auto-labeling PRs
- Add labeler workflow to auto-label on PR
- Add NuGet caching to build-test.yml
- NotifyTeamsTask for Teams webhook
- Fix hardcoded email in deploy.yml

Phase 5: Cleanup
- Update CI/CD documentation
- Create Cake documentation
- Deprecate Development.Napman
- Remove PowerShell scripts (after validation)
- Remove deploy label handling from workflows

---

NEW FILES (~18)
- src/Build/Build.csproj
- src/Build/Program.cs
- src/Build/BuildContext.cs
- src/Build/BuildLifetime.cs
- src/Build/build-config.json
- src/Build/Tasks/*.cs (8-10 task files)
- src/Build/Versioning/*.cs
- .github/labeler.yml
- .github/workflows/labeler.yml
- docs/dev/deployment/cake-build.md

MODIFIED FILES (~5)
- .github/workflows/build-test.yml (add caching)
- .github/workflows/deploy.yml (workflow_dispatch + Cake)
- src/Applications.Octopus/app.manifest.json
- src/Common.Applications/AppVersion.cs
- docs/dev/deployment/ci-cd.md

DEPRECATED
- scripts/deploy/*.ps1
- scripts/build_napman.ps1
- src/Development.Napman/
- deploy: label convention

---

RISK MITIGATION
- Parallel operation: Keep PowerShell scripts during migration
- Feature flags: USE_CAKE=true environment variable
- Rollback: Revert to PowerShell by removing flag
- Incremental: Each phase independently deployable

PR STRATEGY
1. PR 1: Phases 1-2 (Foundation + Core Tasks)
2. PR 2: Phase 3 (Retriever/Taipan support)
3. PR 3: Phase 4 (GitHub Actions + labeler)
4. PR 4: Phase 5 (Cleanup) - after validation period

title: Modernise CI/CD with Cake
description:
Comprehensive modernisation of the CI/CD system, migrating from PowerShell + napman to **Cake Build** (C# Make).

## Key Decision
Full migration to Cake Frosting, replacing both PowerShell scripts and the custom napman CLI.

## Why Cake?
- Cross-platform build automation with C# DSL
- Strong typing and IDE support (IntelliSense, debugging)
- Unit testable build tasks
- Cake v6.0.0 supports .NET 8/9/10
- Reuse existing Common.Applications logic (CalVer, manifests)

## Phase 1: Foundation
- Create Cake Frosting project (src/Build/)
- Create BuildContext with shared settings
- Port CalVer logic (reuse Common.Applications.AppVersion)
- Port manifest logic (reuse Common.Applications.AppManifest)
- Create externalised config (build-config.json)

## Phase 2: Core Tasks
- UpdateVersionTask (replaces napman update)
- BuildTask (replaces build.ps1)
- PackageTask (replaces package.ps1)
- UploadTask (replaces upload.ps1 + proper error handling)
- CollectManifestsTask (replaces napman collect)
- DeployTask (orchestrates all above)

## Phase 3: Application-Specific Tasks
- VelopackPackageTask for Retriever
- MultiVersionBuildTask for Taipan (Revit 2023-2026)
- MultiVersionPackageTask for Taipan

## Phase 4: CI/CD Integration
- Update deploy.yml to use Cake
- Add NuGet caching to build-test.yml
- NotifyTeamsTask for Teams webhook
- PostPRCommentTask for deployment status
- Fix hardcoded email in deploy.yml

## Phase 5: Cleanup
- Update CI/CD documentation
- Create Cake documentation
- Deprecate Development.Napman
- Remove PowerShell scripts (after validation)
- Refactor region blocks in AppVersion.cs

## New Files (~16)
- src/Build/Build.csproj
- src/Build/Program.cs
- src/Build/BuildContext.cs
- src/Build/BuildLifetime.cs
- src/Build/build-config.json
- src/Build/Tasks/*.cs (8-10 task files)
- src/Build/Versioning/*.cs
- docs/dev/deployment/cake-build.md

## Modified Files (~6)
- .github/workflows/build-test.yml
- .github/workflows/deploy.yml
- src/Applications.Octopus/app.manifest.json
- src/Common.Applications/AppVersion.cs
- docs/dev/deployment/ci-cd.md

## Risk Mitigation
- Parallel operation: Keep PowerShell scripts during migration
- Feature flags: USE_CAKE=true environment variable
- Rollback: Revert to PowerShell by removing flag
- Incremental: Each phase independently deployable

## PR Strategy
1. PR 1: Phases 1-2 (Foundation + Core Tasks)
2. PR 2: Phase 3 (Retriever/Taipan support)
3. PR 3: Phase 4 (GitHub Actions integration)
4. PR 4: Phase 5 (Cleanup) - after validation period

## Verification Plan

### Phase 1-2 Validation
```bash
dotnet build src/Build
dotnet run --project src/Build -- --target=UpdateVersion --app=Applications.Octopus --dry-run
dotnet run --project src/Build -- --target=Deploy --app=Applications.Octopus --mod=dev --dry-run
```

### Phase 3 Validation
```bash
dotnet run --project src/Build -- --target=Deploy --app=Applications.Taipan --dry-run
dotnet run --project src/Build -- --target=Deploy --app=Applications.Retriever --dry-run
```

### Phase 4 Validation
1. Create test PR with deploy:Applications.Octopus-dev label
2. Verify Cake runs successfully in GitHub Actions
3. Verify Teams notification received
4. Verify PR comment added with deployment status
5. Verify NuGet caching improves build time

title: Implement reinforcement splitting in geometry
description: Core geometry logic for splitting reinforcement bars.

title: Pull reinforcement detail items from Revit model and split them
description:
Create a Revit command in Applications.Taipan that does the following:

- Select reinforcement bar detail items and floors in the Revit UI.
- Convert these into abstract reinforcement bars as per the Applications.Taipan.Geometry library. Extract the parameters listed below from the detail item.
- Convert the floors into abstract floor geometry as per the Applications.Taipan.Geometry library.
- Use the splitting algorithm to split each bar into individual bars that respect the floor geometry.
- Convert the abstract geometry back into new bars in Revit and delete the previous bar if a bar was indeed split.

The following parameters (in addition to the normal geometric parameters) should be extracted from the parameters in Revit:
- Reinforcement Group: a string, either "BTM" or "TOP". If "BOTTOM" change to "BTM". Always make it upper case regardless of the initial case. If neither is found, don't do anything with this bar but provide a warning to the user.
- Bar Number: an integer number (the number of bars)
- Bar Dia.: a decimal length (the diameter of the bar)
- Bar Extra: a string
- Assigned Extent Id: a string GUID used to identify the corresponding extent value
- Extent: The extent of the bars (typically extracted using the extent parameter)
- Spacing of Bars: a decimal length (the spacing between bars)

The Reinforcement Group should be used to determine the projection surface in the splitting algorithm, and all other properties should be included in the abstract detail item as properties that are then copied to the new bars that are created.

The existing Reinforcement commands (AddPerimeterBar and AssignReinforcementExtents) already perform most of the extraction and creation logic. Refactor these to a single service or set of extension methods that can be used for the existing and future reinforcement-based commands.

title: Split bars placed by the placement commands
description: Apply the same splitting logic to bars that have been placed by the reinforcement placement commands (AddPerimeterBar). The IReinforcementBar elements should be created first then split as abstract geometry before being turned back into detail components in Revit. This pattern should generally be used for all Revit commands - first convert to an abstract form, process the abstract forms of the item then converted back into the Revit item. Add this to a CLAUDE.md in the Applications.Taipan folder.

description: Automatically create and position tags for reinforcement bars.

title: Improve lapping behaviour
description: Enhance lapping behaviour by adding support line awareness for smarter bar splitting.

title: Split lines at top and bottom of extrusion unions
description:
Split lines at the top and bottom of extrusion unions. This should be done in the Common.Geometry library so that we can later use it in the Taipan application with reinforcement bars.

When a line crosses through an extrusion, a setting of top or bottom should be applied. If top, it should be assumed to be projected onto the top surface of the extrusion union. It should then be split at all of the top "steps" in the extrusion (which are already being detected in another method). A similar task should be performed for "bottom" lines.

title: Implement reinforcement with extension and termination logic depending on intersection types
description:
Use the split line by extrusion group behaviour that was implemented previously in the Common.Geometry library as the basis for a new command "SplitBar" in the Taipan library. This should follow the same pattern of using the abstract reinforcement bar representation to pull the Revit geometry into simplified two dimensional bars, then apply the split bar algorithm to the abstract geometry (such that it is testable) before converting back to Revit geometry and modifying the Revit model.

First, create the abstract version of this process with tests, similar to how the reinforcement and extent matching algorithm works. A bar should be designated as either "top" or "bottom" and should be selected in the Revit model. Then, the "top" or "bottom" bars should be split as appropriate by the algorithm being performed. 

This command should also handle bar extension and termination behaviour based on the type of intersection encountered (e.g. with an step or edge). Tag the ends of each segment after the split with information on the termination *type* that was encountered. This is directional - the terminations should be applied at both the start and end of a bar, with the meaning changing slightly for some of the termination types depending on whether they are at the start or end. Given that the bars that are being split are single bars, the resulting bar segments will have the same normal vector. The list of bar segments should therefore be kept in order such that the end termination of a bar in the list is at the same intersection point of the start termination of the next bar (unless clipped at a hole), although the termination types don't necessarily need to match. 

Each termination will also have a "TerminationDetail" applied, which is the physical modification at the end of the bar to suit the condition that it is in.

TerminationTypes:

- Free: if a bar starts or ends within a floor slab, the start termination of the start segment will be "free" and/or the end termination of the end segment will be "free". Nothing is done regarding the termination.
- SlabEdgeTop: when a top bar encounters the edge of a slab. The bar should have a "CogUp" (i.e. 90 degree hook downwards) at the end, with the length set by the thickness of the slab.
- SlabEdgeBottom: when a bottom bar encounters the edge of a slab. The bar should have a "CogDown" termination detail (i.e. 90 degree hook upwards) at the end, with the length of the cog set by the thickness of the slab.
- TopStepUp: A step in the top of the slab going up in the direction of the bar - i.e. the bar that *ends* with this termination is lower than the bar that *starts* with this termination. Only top reinforcement can encounter this. The bar before the termination should have the termination detail set to "FullLap", and the bar after the termination should have the termination detail set to "CogDownZ", with the length of the cog set by the total thickness of the slab.
- TopStepDown: A step in the top of the slab going down in the direction of the bar. The termination details should be applied to the opposite bars as the TopStepUp termination type.
- BottomStepUp: A step in the bottom of the slab going up in the direction of the bar - i.e. the bar that *ends* with this termination is lower than the bar that *starts* with this termination. Only bottom reinforcement can encounter this. The bar before the termination should have the termination detail set to "CogUpZ", and the bar after the termination should have the termination detail set to "FullLap", with the length of the cog set by the total thickness of the slab.
- BottomStepDown. A step in the top of the slab going down in the direction of the bar. The termination details should be applied to the opposite bars as the BottomStepUp termination type.

Note on termination types: When setting the termination details according to the termination types, the details mentioned above are "defaults" that should be applied only if there is room for the detail type to fit as these often run past the step. For example, a 12mm bar may have a 500mm lap length, so if meets a TopStepUp and has a FullLap applied but there is a slab edge just 300mm away, the bar should still continue past the step but instead of the FullLap it should have a CogDown applied as it must cog down at the slab edge. To do this, a length should be calculated for all TerminationDetails. If the length cannot be achieved in the space that exists between the intersection point and a step/edge that would block the bar, the bar should just continue and treat the step/edge as a SlabEdge* termination type.

TerminationDetails:

Each of these termination details also includes some metadata - they should be a new set of types that all implement some IBarTermination interface.

Additional end characteristics should be applied to bars given the following splitting locations:
- CogUp: A 90 degree bend up
- CogUpZ: A 90 degree bend up that ends in a flattened length equal to a "standard" cog length
- CogDown: A 90 degree bend down
-CogDownZ: A 90 degree bend down that ends in a flattened length equal to a "standard" cog length
- FullLap: A full lap extension of the bar past the end point of the segment.
- HalfLap: Half of a lap extension (not used for these TerminationTypes but will be used later.

The "standard" laps and cogs will need to be calculated based on the bar sizes and concrete details. For now, create methods that take this information in as parameters but otherwise just return 150mm for cogs and 600mm for laps.

title: Implement lap offsets
description:
Implement lap offset calculations to stagger laps between adjacent bars. This should be both an algorithm that is applied to split bars as well as one that can be applied to any selected bars. The basic principle is that when a bar is split it generates a series of concentric lines that are end to end on plan. To display visually in 2D, every second bar is offset a particular distance.

This functionality should take a collection of bars and work out which bars are collinear and end-to-end. It should then sort the bars into a line and make a LapOffset property true for every second bar. The Revit item itself will handle the rendering of the detail item object, but in the tests we should implement this offset rendering ourselves.

There should also be a Similar property Boolean property at each end. If the bar is similar to the following bar (i.e. the bar diameter and spacing is equal), the similar property should be turned on. In structural engineering drawings this should be drawn as a loop that links both bars. This is handled by the Revit detail item based on a Yes/No parameter, but we should also be rendering this approximately in our test library.

title: Migrate from Serilog.Sinks.ApplicationInsights to OpenTelemetry SDK
description: The current logging infrastructure uses Serilog.Sinks.ApplicationInsights to send telemetry to Azure Application Insights. - `src/Common.Applications/ApplicationCollection.cs`

title: Map all parameters between detail item and reinforcement bars
description:
There are a number of parameters that need to be mapped from the detail items in Revit to the IReinforcementBar class. These are as follows based on the parameter name:

## Parameters

Example:
- Parameter Name (Type): status

Already mapped:
- Reinforcement Group (string)
- Bar Number (integer)
- Bar Extra (string)

Not mapped:
- Bar Dia. (decimal length): Doesn't appear to be mapped correctly, when a bar is split the diameter isn't copied to the new bars.
- Start Bar Type (ElementID): The element ID of the detail item type that is used to display the start of the bar. See below for information on the various types here.
- Start Flip Direction (boolean): Indicates whether the termination detail at the start of the bar is mirrored.
- Start Loop (boolean): Indicates whether a visual loop that denotes whether two bars have the same diameter and spacing.
- Start Continuous (boolean): Indicates whether the bar should visually extend all the way to the start point or should be shown stopping 100mm short (for visual effect).
- End Bar Type: As above, but for the end of the bar.
- End Flip Direction: As above, but for the end of the bar.
- End Loop: As above, but for the end of the bar.
- End Continuous: As above, but for the end of the bar.

## Start Bar Type and End Bar Type

These relate to the termination details used in the Applications.Taipan.Geometry library. The type IDs should be looked up by name from the model when the command is run - they relate to types of the "End Bar" family, which is included in models as a NestedFamilyTypeReference. The type names are as follows, mapped to their corresponding termination details:

-  "Default": "Free" - this should also set the "Start Continuous" or "End Continuous" to be true if the bar end is located in the middle of the slab and hasn't been clipped at an edge or is located at an edge.
- "End 'L' Bar" : "CogUp" or "CogDown" - note that whether it is "CogUp" or "CogDown" can be changed with the "Flip Direction" parameter.
- "End 'Z' Bar": "CogUpZ" or "CogDownZ"
- "Lap - Overlap": "FullLap"
- "Lap - Standard": "HalfLap"

There are also several types that are not yet represented - these will be included later so just add a placeholder for them in the mapping.

- "End Cranked Bar"
- "Lapped 'L' Bar"
- "Lapped 'U' Bar"
- "Lapped 'Z' Bar"
- "Stair"
- "Stair Lapped"

## Rotation and flipping

Generally, when drawn horizontally from left to right, the default L and Z bar positioning is "CogDown" (i.e. towards negative Y). When drawn right to left the positioning is "CogUp". When drawn top to bottom it is drawn "CogUp" (i.e. with the L bar tips facing to the left) and when drawn bottom to top it is drawn "CogDown" (i.e. with the L bar tips facing to the right. The position in Revit is therefore fixed and rotates with the bars. Consider this when mapping the bar types and flip directions.

## Loops and laps

The loops that show the same diameter and spacing are drawn "below" the bar, such that in the lapping algorithm where every bar is drawn either in it's original location or 100mm "up", the loops should be added to the bars that are moved. Currently in the rendering algorithm a boolean parameter sets the position of the bar and the rendering algorithm just offsets the bar. In Revit the actual coordinates of the start and end points must be adjusted when the bar is offset for "lapped" bars.

title: Migrate Applications.Retriever to Full DI Logging
description:
Migrate Applications.Retriever from the legacy hybrid logging pattern to use the standard `AddNorthropLogging()` DI extension.

## Current State

Applications.Retriever currently uses a hybrid pattern:
1. Initializes logging asynchronously via `NorthropLoggerFactory.InitializeAsync()`
2. Manually bridges the static factory into DI: `collection.AddSingleton(NorthropLoggerFactory.GetLoggerFactory())`

This is the legacy pattern that predates the modern DI-based logging refactor.

## Migration Plan

1. **Replace InitializeLoggingAsync()** with synchronous `AddNorthropLogging()` configuration:
   ```csharp
   services.AddNorthropLogging(config =>
   {
       config.ApplicationName = "Applications.Retriever";
       config.EnableConsoleLogging = enableConsole;
       config.MinimumFileLevel = LogLevel.Debug;
       config.SkipAuthentication = skipAuthentication;
   });
   ```

2. **Move authentication to Startup event** - Use `AddNorthropAuthentication()` and call `IAuthenticationService.AuthenticateAsync()` separately

3. **Remove manual logger factory bridging** - Delete:
   ```csharp
   collection.AddSingleton(NorthropLoggerFactory.GetLoggerFactory());
   collection.AddSingleton(typeof(ILogger<>), typeof(Logger<>));
   ```

4. **Update ServiceCollectionExtensions.AddAppServices()** - Include the full DI logging chain

5. **Update shutdown** - Use `INorthropLoggingLifetime.Shutdown()` instead of `NorthropLoggerFactory.Shutdown()`

## Key Files

- `src/Applications.Retriever/App.axaml.cs` (lines 111-153, 158-189, 257-262)
- `src/Applications.Retriever/ServiceCollectionExtensions.cs`

## Complexity

Medium - requires careful handling of async authentication vs sync DI container build

## Why This Is Needed

Applications.Retriever is the primary consumer of Common.Applications. Without full DI support in Retriever, there is no way to properly test Common.Applications logging changes.

title: Add beams
description:
The current commands in Applications.Taipan relating to geometry only use Floor elements from Revit to map to Extrusions. We also need to account for beams, which are linear elements with a width and thickness.

The structural beam families in Revit also include other types of non-concrete framing that would not affect reinforcement bars. To avoid including these, consider only the beams that are in the Structural Framing category and have a StructuralMaterialType = Concrete and StructuralType = Beam. They are of the FamilyInstance class.

See here for relevant documentation: https://www.revitapidocs.com/2016/0d2231f8-91e6-794f-92ae-16aad8014b27.htm

Try to read the following parameters of these elements to determine the geometry:

Instance parameters (including definition name):
- y Justification "Y_JUSTIFICATION"
- y Offset Value: "Y_OFFSET_VALUE"
- yz Justification: "YZ_JUSTIFICATION"
- z Justification: "Z_JUSTIFICATION"
- z Offset Value: "Z_OFFSET_VALUE"
- Start Level Offset: "STRUCTURAL_BEAM_END0_ELEVATION"
- End Level Offset: "STRUCTURAL_BEAM_END1_ELEVATION"
- Elevation at Top: "STRUCTURAL_ELEVATION_AT_TOP"
- Cross-Section Rotation: "STRUCTURAL_BEND_DIR_ANGLE"

These instance parameters are mostly to understand the minor modifications to the geometry that can be made through parameters. For now we should only support flat surfaces - i.e. just flag a warning if a beam has a cross section rotation not equal to a multiple of 90 degrees or has different offset values at each end.

Type parameters (from the related FamilySymbol class parameters)
- Beam Depth
- Beam Width

These type parameters are not built in parameters. They can be used to determine the depth and width of the beam. The orientation of the beam should be determined from the Location property of the FamilyInstance.

The beams should be converted into an Extrusion so that they can be considered along with the rest of the geometry as part of the floor geometry.

If not all of these values can be determined, the instance should just be ignored. As with the floors, all visible elements from a view should be used if nothing is selected.

title: Add shafts
description:
The current commands in Applications.Taipan relating to geometry only use Floor elements from Revit to map to Extrusions. We also need to account for openings (which cut holes in floors.

See here for documentation: https://www.revitapidocs.com/2016/720de864-9f4e-c606-c7f4-2e7a0b2da46f.htm

Holes are already considered in floors when they are included in the overall floor profile, but openings can behave a little differently as they may overlap with more than one floor. I would suggest treating these differently by first retrieving the profiles of all the elements through their Sketch.Profile property (which is a CurveArrArray), then using this closed polygon to subtract from the layers as the ExtrusionUnion is generated. This would act something like a "void" extrusion.

This should only apply to openings in the category "Shaft Openings". The top and bottom levels can be determined from the Z values in the BoundingBox (unless there is a better way to determine this), and additional cuts should be applied at the top and bottom of the shafts (if appropriate). A shaft can technically create a top step in a slab if the bottom level does not extend all the way through the full extrusion.

Implement this as a VoidExtrusion in the Common.Geometry library with tests for various hole arrangements in the Common.Geometry tests. This should then be mapped through to Opening elements in the Applications.Taipan project, and various hole arrangements should be added to the Applications.Taipan.Geometry tests to ensure that they work with various reinforcement arrangements.

title: Allow selection of export formats
description: When using the OutgoingDrawings command of Applications.Taipan, a set of DWG and PDF drawings are produced, along with a transmittal in Excel. Instead of doing this immediately, add a dialog window after the command button is pressed that allows the user to select which of these to export to the outgoing drawings folder. At least one option must be selected, This can just be done with check boxes. The window styling should match the other windows in the Applications.Taipan project.

title: Update Sunset interpreter for .sunmd files
description: Review the Sunset Markdown specification and the Sunset ROADMAP in the docs folder. Review the current implementation of the Sunset Markdown parser and plan to update it to match the Sunset Markdown specification. Create unit and integration tests to confirm that simple examples of a Sunset Markdown file including Sunset code is successfully interpreted into a Markdown file with LaTeX outputs.

title: Add output for .sunmd using Avalonia Markdown component and xaml-math
description:
There is currently a SunFileTool that contains an edit and preview panel for editing and showing a preview of executed Sunset code, and a MarkdownFileTool that contains the same for Markdown code.

Add a new SunMarkdownFileTool that contains the same, but for the Sunset Markdown format (.sunmd) that is described in the reference file in docs/user/sunset/. This should also show interpreter errors, similar to the SunFileTool.

title: Add output for Markdown files using Avalonia Markdown component
description: Create a new file tool for opening Markdown (.md) files. This should have a side-by-side edit and preview panels, similar to the SunFileTool where the edit tab is implemented using Monaco. The preview panel should use the Avalonia Markdown component that I believe is currently used in the chat window.

title: Use xaml-math rather than KaTeX in embedded browser
description: Instead of using KaTeX in an embedded browser for the display of the rendered output from the sunset tool in the SunFileTool of Octopus, I would like to instead try using the xaml-math library (see https://github.com/ForNeVeR/xaml-math) to render the LaTeX natively and display it in the preview panel. Could you please update this?

title: Release Taipan with reinforcement tools
Remove obsolete reinforcement methods and update tests
description: In the Applications.Taipan and Applications.Taipan.Geometry projects there are a number of classes and methods that were made obsolete when we refactored to a service based architecture in PR #571. Delete these classes and methods, then fix all tests and implementations to use the new service based architecture instead. Build and run all tests, then create a PR when complete.

title: Update geometry export to include shafts and beams
description:
Shafts and beams were added to Applications.Taipan and Applications.Taipan.Geometry in PR #578 and #574. Add both of these to the geometry export command such that they can be imported as fixtures in tests. Export them as separate objects to the floors and reinforcement bars that are currently being exported.

Beams are currently supported by converting to IFloorGeometry elements with the BeamGeometryAdapter. Instead of this, add a new LinearExtrusion that takes in the basic properties of a beam (start and end points, width, depth and top level) and converts this to a normal Extrusion element (Extrusion, LinearExtrusion and VoidExtrusion should inherit from the same interface such that the ExtrusionUnion is just created from a collection of IExtrusions).

Finally, update the tests in Common.Geometry such that the rendering of the resulting ExtrusionUnions are simplified to show extrusion unions similar to how they are shown in the Applications.Taipan.Geometry tests, with thick red outlines, thin red holes, green top steps and dashed green bottom steps. Use the same rendering method for both the Applications.Taipan.Geometry tests and the Common.Geometry tests.

title: Add tests for bar splitting with prototype slab fixture
description: Add tests for the BarSplittingService in the Applications.Taipan.Geometry.Test project that uses the test fixture prototype-l1-slab.json. This should place horizontal and vertical bars in various interesting locations throughout the slab (both top and bottom) to confirm that the correct number and placement of bars is generated. Include a test render for each test.

title: Fix bar rendering for lapped end bars
description:
PR #575 introduced lapped L bars for when bars have "bent" terminations at each end - one end should have a "lapped" L-bar. The rendering appears to be incorrect for this case, as it just looks like a CogUpZ or CogDownZ. Instead of this, there should be a straight length equal to the "lap length" of the bar (which is currently defaulting to 600mm), that is parallel to the bar and placed at a 100mm offset. This bar should end in line with the end of the bar and start 600mm back towards the bar, thus lapping with it. The end of this additional bar should have a cog at the end of it that is the same dimension as the other bar. This "L" bar should be visually completely detached from the main straight segment of the bar.

Make this fix for rendering both the lapped L bars and lapped Z bars.

title: Add knowledge management architecture diagrams
description:
Install the likec4 CLI tool first with `npm i -D likec4`.

Complete the C4 architecture diagrams. Add sequence diagrams for key workflows (authentication, document read/write, agent operations, publishing). Extend deployment diagram to include agent container and Azure Blob Storage for Git LFS.

**Files to update:**
- `docs/arch/apps/knowledgeManager.c4` - Add sequence diagrams
- `docs/arch/systems/knowledgeManagement.c4` - Add agent container
- `docs/arch/deployment/deployment.c4` - Add knowledge infrastructure

**Dependencies:** None

title: Add seed data to knowledge repository
description:
Create initial content demonstrating system capabilities. Include sample policies (Markdown), PDF source documents (Git LFS), and interlinked pages using wiki syntax.

**Files:**
- `tools/knowledge-seed/seed-data/` - Sample Markdown files
- `tools/knowledge-seed/seed.ps1` - Seeding script
- `tools/knowledge-seed/README.md` - Documentation

**Sample Content:**
- Company policies (WHS, IT, Leave)
- Project templates
- Staff CV templates
- Interlinked knowledge pages with `[[Wiki Links]]`

**Dependencies:** AxdMfI, QhZl6N complete

title: Create librarian web application
Fix loops for lapped bars
description:
In the Taipan application lapped bars have a property for adding a "loop" symbol at the ends of the bars that indicate that the adjacent bar is similar. This is currently working in the split command by adding this loop to any end that is split with an adjacent bar. However, in Revit this creates two loops at a point where the bars overlap, which is not correct - the lap should only be added to one end.

The lap loop symbol tends to point downwards (in the direction of the CogDown) and respects the flipping parameter. At each "lap point" at the end of one bar and the start of another, the loop should be applied to the bar that is "above" the other bar.

Add a symbol to the reinforcement bar test renderer so that we can test this behaviour. The symbol should be offset 250mm from the end of the bar (along the length) and 50mm perpendicular to the bar in the "down" direction and respecting the flip.

title: Fix flips for lapped terminations
description: When we have lapped terminations, e.g. CogUpZLapped, that were introduced in PR #575 for preventing bent terminations on both ends of a bar, this should be flipped in accordance with the same algorithm that would otherwise flip the non-lapped version that we had before. I was finding that the flip was not being applied to the end of bottom bars at a step up (where a lapped CogUpZ was applied), where previously this was being applied properly for normal CogUpZ bars.

title: Fix offsets for laps with lapped terminations
Review specification for .sunmd files
description: There is a specification for Sunset Markdown files in docs/dev/Sunset. Please review it in relation to the language reference in docs/user/sunset. Let me know if you have any questions about how the Sunset Markdown specification would work when the interpreter is executed to convert a Sunset Markdown file into a regular Markdown file with the results of the Sunset calculations. Let me know if you have any suggestions for additional features to be added, and update the Fleece issue for implementing .sunmd files that is a sibling to this one.

title: Automate fee proposal generation
Create client web application
description:
Create React SPA with TypeScript using Vite. Implements file explorer, Markdown editor with Obsidian-style wiki links, search interface, and agent chat.

**Project Structure:**
```
src/Web.Knowledge.Client/
├── src/
│   ├── auth/AuthProvider.tsx (MSAL)
│   ├── api/ (client, repositories, documents, search)
│   ├── components/ (Layout, FileExplorer, Editor, Search, Chat)
│   ├── pages/ (Home, Repository, Document, Search)
│   └── hooks/ (useAuth, useRepository, useSearch)
└── staticwebapp.config.json
```

**Key Features:**
- MSAL.js authentication with Entra ID
- Tree view file explorer with drag-drop upload
- Split-view Markdown editor with wiki link support `[[Page Name]]`
- Semantic search with faceted results
- Agent chat with streaming responses via SignalR

**Tech Stack:** React 18, TypeScript, Vite, TailwindCSS, TanStack Query, Monaco Editor, MSAL.js

**Dependencies:** AxdMfI substantially complete

title: Create infrastructure deployment scripts
description:
Create Bicep templates and GitHub Actions for deploying knowledge management infrastructure to Azure.

**Infrastructure:**
- Azure Container Apps Environment (`cae-knowledge`)
- Knowledge Server container (1-10 instances, 1 CPU, 2Gi memory)
- Forgejo container (single instance, persistent volume)
- Postgres Flexible Server (pgvector enabled)
- Static Web App (Knowledge Browser)

**Files:**
```
infra/knowledge/
├── main.bicep
├── modules/
│   ├── container-app-environment.bicep
│   ├── knowledge-server.bicep
│   ├── forgejo.bicep
│   ├── postgres.bicep
│   └── static-web-app.bicep
└── parameters/ (dev.bicepparam, prod.bicepparam)
```

**Workflow:** `.github/workflows/deploy-knowledge.yml`

title: Add rooms to geometry export
description: Update the export slab command to include exporting selected rooms. Refer to the documentation at https://www.revitapidocs.com/2026/75c9d2c7-a402-ea8b-9e7c-f8bc3510bbd5.htm for information about the room class and how to extract the outline and level geometry from the object. Change the name of the export slab command to "ExportGeometry"

title: Add channels for Retriever deployment
Include shafts in geometry that splits reinforcement
description: The split reinforcement command in the Applications.Taipan app doesn't include the shaft geometry as a void in the extrusion union that is used to split reinforcement. The prototype L1 slab fixture contains a shaft in it, ensure that this is added to the extrusion union that is used for the splitting service tests and update the tests and command to ensure shafts are included.

title: Generate fee proposal documents in Typst
Add agent prompts for proposal generation
Remove extent lines from parallel perimeter bars
description: When parallel perimeter bars are created using the "add perimeter bar" command, very short extent lines are created perpendicular to the edges. The creation of these should be optional, add a boolean switch to add these that defaults to false (don't create them). We will update the UI to allow control of this switch later.

title: Retriever fails to install multiple applications simultaneously
description:
When I click on the Update all button or try to install multiple applications at once using the Retriever application, often only one application will be installed (typically the first one clicked) and the others won't be.

The installation of applications should be fully asynchronous, so multiple applications installs at once should be possible. Review this and come up with a fix.

Also, improve the output in the display to show more information about the download status (e.g. percentage downloaded) and the installation (extracting, unzipping, copying).

title: Add perimeter bars around internal void openings
description: Currently perimeter bars are created around the external perimeter of the ExtrusionUnion objects generated from the floor geometry. They should also be generated around the internal perimeters that are created by the shaft geometry.

title: Cog parallel perimeter bars inwards and add extensions
description:
When the AddPerimeterBars command is run, the BarSplittingService is run to split bars. This creates cogs at the end of every bar that terminates at an edge.

After this step is run, an additional correction step should be run (just for the creation of parallel perimeter bars) that does the following:

- For bars that have cogs at the perimeter, the cogs should be turned inwards regardless of whether they are top or bottom bars.
- For bars that have free terminations (i.e. do not terminate at an edge but stop at re-entrant corners), these should have full lap extensions applied to them.

title: Add controls for bar size, number, spacing and layer
description:
Currently when the AddPerimeterBar command is run, a message box pops up asking the user to confirm whether they want to add the reinforcement as top or bottom. Instead of this, I want to add the following controls to the Revit ribbon to control the algorithm:

- Drop down to select layer (top or bottom)
- Drop down to select perpendicular bar size (12 (default), 16, 20, 24, 28, 32, 36, 40)
- Numeric input for perpendicular bar spacing (default to 200mm, display in mm)
- Drop down to select parallel bar size (same as above for perpendicular bars)
- Numeric input to select perpendicular bar spacing (default to 100mm, display in mm)
- Numeric input to select perpendicular bar number (default to 2)

Group these appropriately. Refer to this article for information on adding Ribbon controls: https://help.autodesk.com/view/RVT/2025/ENU/?guid=Revit_API_Revit_API_Developers_Guide_Introduction_Add_In_Integration_Ribbon_Panels_and_Controls_html

title: Complete logging update
Replace MockTestLogger with TestContextLogger
description: When building there are some "Obsolete" build warnings that are shown due to the use of the MockTestLogger. Delete the MockTestLogger type, then find these instances and use the TestContextLogger instead as suggested.

title: Improve Retriever app installation
Remove old versions of applications
description:
The Retriever application keeps old versions of applications when a new one is updated. This is no longer required, just rename the installed application folder to allow rollback if the installation fails, then move this folder to the "bin" when the new application installation is complete and the new contents are verified.

In doing so, add a clean up service that cleans up all items in the bin that were moved there more then 7 days ago.

title: Add uninstall and reinstall capabilities
Always run special installation actions even if app is installed
description: When an application is installed in Retriever, if the application already exists the "special installation instructions" that should follow aren't run. Always run the special installation instructions again regardless of whether the application is already installed.

title: Use Velopack for Octopus
Use normal cog directions for parallel perimeter bars
description:
Pull all changes from main first.

PR #605 corrected the cog directions of parallel perimeter bars to be inwards instead of the direction that is generated due to the layering of the bar. Revert this so that the cog directions are the same direction as expected due to the layering of the bar.

Also, remove the full lap extension from the end of the bar that extends past re-entrant corners, and instead move the node so the bar extends past the corner the same length as the perpendicular bar length.

The perpendicular bar length should be configurable as well, make this a parameter that can be changed in the generation options.

title: Fix broken bar to extent matching
description: The bar to extent matching in Applications.Taipan has stopped working as it used to, likely around the time that a major refactoring was performed in #571. It used to match intersecting bars and extents, but when I recently tested it in Revit it didn't identify a match between a bar and an extent that were clearly intersecting. Run the tests and review the results for this feature and try to identify any issues. The issue may be in the conversion of Revit geometry to abstract geometry.

title: Improve Octopus file tools
Add file and folder watch
description: The folders and files that are shown in the Octopus file explorer panel should be watched for changes to ensure that if there are any updates in the system that the application also updates the view. Consider how something like VS Code does this.

title: Add cut, copy, paste, delete and rename tools
Remove LatexPreview and use Katex and Markdown rendering for MarkdownMathPreview
description:
We previously replaced KaTeX in a web viewer (in the KatexPreview control) with AvaloniaMath in the LatexPreview control (see PR #583), and then implemented the MarkdownMathPreview in PR #609 using interleaved Markdown and AvaloniaMath controls.

I have found the display characteristics of the AvalaniaMath control to be lacking, so would like to revert back to a single web-based viewer. Remove the LatexPreview and MarkdownMathPreview controls, and upgrade the KatexPreview control so that it handles Markdown with math blocks (rendered with KaTeX).

title: Release Octopus
Fix issues identified by Sustainability team
Fix page navigation when single page is returned by query
description: When the Parrot agent reads a file and uses it to respond to a prompt, if a single page is used within a file clicking on the page opens the PDF viewer but doesn't successfully navigate to the correct page. The exact page selection seems to work OK when multiple pages are present and the user can click between them - this seems to indicate a race condition, please review and prepare a fix.

title: Show link to relevant files alongside relevant chat
description: The Octopus application Parrot agent chat window currently has a separate tab for the files that have been used in the response. Also add these links to the files at the end of the chat message response. This should be in a dropdown that can be expanded and collapsed with a summary "5 files referenced" in small text.

title: Add button to open file in native application
description:
For all files that are used by the Parrot agent in Octopus, this is currently returned as a large button that can be clicked to open the file in the preview panel. Only provide this for file types that can be opened in the Octopus preview panel, and add a button within the control that opens the file using the native application (e.g. Word for .docx files). I believe Avalonia has a "launcher" service for this.

The native application button should be shown for all files regardless whether they can be shown in the local preview.

title: Identify pages that have images for double checking
Add document OCR
Speed up document content search
Add metadata support into Sunset
description: The Sunset language has some limited support for metadata like a description, references, etc. Refer to the language reference in docs/user/sunset. Review this syntax to ensure that it won't cause clashes and that it is essentially a key-value dictionary that allows arbitrary keys as well as a handful of predefined ones like descriptions and references. Update the parser and interpreter so it can handle this metadata.

title: Automatic formatting
description:
Add automatic formatting to the Sunset parser by creating a visitor that pretty prints the AST back into code. All of the required information to do this should be available in the AST, which also now contains trivia.

Add a format command to the Sunset CLI.

Use an 80 character maximum line width. Refer to the @docs/user/sunset/reference.md for indentation rules.

title: Add trivia to parser
description: In the Sunset parser @src/Sunset.Parser/Parsing/Parser.cs all trivia (i.e. comments) as either trivia before or after definitions, similar to how this is handled in the Roslyn parser. I want to eventually use this to allow auto-formatting by rebuilding the code from the AST, but for now just add the trivia to the objects.

title: Allow arbitrary metadata keys
description: For the Sunset language, currently the only metadata keys accepted are predefined keywords for "description", "symbol", etc. I would like to change this to allow arbitrary keys like "control" - these can be kept in a dictionary of metadata rather than directly in the definition object.

title: Extend perimeter parallel bars past re-entrant corners the same length as the perpendicular bars
Ensure all lapping offsets are inwards from the slab edge for perimeter bars
description:
When laps are created in parallel perimeter bars with the bar splitter service, the offset direction for laps should be adjusted such that it is always away from the slab edge or void edge. That is, if there are no steps the offset should alternate between 200mm and 300mm from the edge as opposed to 200mm and 100mm as can happen sometimes with the current algorithm. If steps are involved these can be treated as normal.

Consider whether this should be a separate stage or a modification to the bar splitter service.

title: Fix assignment of parameters after perimeter bars are generated
description: When perimeter bars are generated, the parameters (bar diameter, spacing, number, layer, etc.) don't seem to be set in the reinforcement detail items in Revit. Investigate whether this is due to the bar properties not being set for the reinforcement bar data, or whether the properties are not being set when the Revit detail item is created by the adapter. Then, plan to create a fix.

title: Assign bar extents after perimeter reinforcement generation
description: After perimeter reinforcement bars are generated, the "match extents" command logic should be run to match bars and extents. This should be configurable through a drop down list in the reinforcement generation options window with the options "match all bars and extents", "match new bars and extents", "don't match bars and extents".

title: Keep record of next and previous bars in chain when new bars are created
description:
I have added two parameters "Adjacent Reinforcement Id A" and "Adjacent Reinforcement Id C" to the reinforcement detail items in Revit. "A" represents the start of the bar and "C" represents the end of the bar.

When the BarSplittingService splits bars, these parameters should be populated with the IDs of the adjacent bar segments. If it is the first or last segment of the bar, it should be empty.

Note that BarNumber is the property that should be used to keep track of the index of the bar in the list of split segments, BarCount is the number of bars that are represented by the bar detail item.

Note that this ID is a GUID from a Revit element that is generated in Revit - this may need to be done as the final step after the bar is created, so there will need to be some way of mapping the Revit ID of the actual bar created against the index of the bar in the ReinforcementBarData list, and this parameter set right at the end after the Revit bars are created.

title: Update UI for bar generation
description:
Make the following changes to the dialog 

- Make min edge length an advanced feature, with some explanatory text ("Edges that are shorter than this length will not have perimeter bars added"). There should be an "Advanced settings" group.
- Use group boxes to group the controls. Make the perpendicular and parallel bar groups side by side (i.e. in a grid with two columns).
- Change the "Type" from a select to a check box in the parallel and perpendicular bar groups labelled "Add perimeter bars parallel to edge" and "Add perimeter bars perpendicular to edge". Calculate the type enum based on whether these are selected, and disable the "OK" button if they are both deselected.
- Default to "Include internal openings" being true. Change the text to "Add perimeter bars around voids"
- Make the conversion from mm to feet happen after the ViewModel such that whole numbers are displayed in the UI
- Add a "number" parameter to the parallel bars. Default to 2
- Remove the "offset" parameter from the parallel bars, this should be set manually
- Change "Include parallel extents" to "Add extents for parallel bars"
- Remove "Extent offset" and just set this to 1000mm by default
- Make "Include parallel extents" true by default, but add a note that if the extent calculated is less than 600mm that an extent will not be added. If it is false, never add extents even if the extent calculated is greater than 600mm. Change the text to "Add extents for parallel bars".
- Remove "Extent width" - this should be calculated based on the number of bars and spacing ((number of bars - 1) * spacing).
- When no slabs are selected, just use all visible slabs, beams and shafts as geometry

title: Add calculated bar extension lengths
description:
Move the GetStandardCogHeight and GetStandardLapLength functions from the Applications.Taipan.BarSplitOptions class to a new static class that contains code calculations related to the Australian Standard AS3600 for concrete. Calculate the standard cog length based on the bar size and the lap length based on the concrete strength (default to 40MPa, but we will add this in as a property that is pulled from Revit later) and concrete depth (we will add this in as a property from Revit later but for now just use <300mm depth for bottom bars and >300mm depth for top bars).

Use this to calculate the length of the bar terminations and put this length in the parameters "Additional Length A" and "Additional Length C" (A being the start of the bar and C being the end of the bar) when the Revit detail item is created.

Note that for the cog heights for terminations at slab edges and steps, the cog height should be the total depth of slab at that point and not the "standard" cog height (I believe this is already set in the TerminationDetail objects). The standard cog height should be used for the short extension at the end of the Z bars.

title: Update geometry to separate floor depths for lapped floors
description:
When floor geometry is exported from Revit and merged into an ExtrusionUnion, if two floors are overlapped it currently identifies steps in the top and bottom levels and links back the edges to the original flat extrusions. The thickness of the original flat extrusions is used to drive the height of generated reinforcement bars (for example in the split reinforcement bar command it will use this height value for the height of the cog bar).

I would like to be able to more carefully keep track of the overall thickness of the slabs in these lapped zones. If two slabs are 200mm thick, and one slab is 100mm higher in level than the other slab, in the overlapping zone the overall slab thickness is 300mm. However, with the current method because it is only linked to one of the original slab extrusions the reported height will be 200mm.

Add an intermediate step in the generation of the floor extrusions where overlapping floors that intersect should have these intersections "cut out" of both of the original floors, and replaced with a new extrusion that is the combined height of both floors including their difference in levels (e.g. 300mm thick in the above example, with the top level of the slab set by the top level of the highest intersecting slab).

This extrusion should then be used as part of the extrusion union.

This should happen after the floor geometry is exported from Revit using the export geometry command, as I want the exported floor geometry in test fixtures to accurately match what is in Revit. The steps should be:

- Convert Revit floor geometry to abstract extrusions
- Modify extrusions such that overlapping extrusions become non-overlapping with combined overall thickness (new step)
- Create the extrusion union

There may be a large number of edge cases - create a comprehensive set of tests for this feature with one to three overlapping floors in the same area and use the visual rendering system that has been developed.

title: Fix units in bar generation UI
description:
The units in the bar generation UI were changed in PR #619 to use feet. I would like to use millimetres for all dimensions instead of feet, the problem was that when the default feet values were converted to millimetres the values were rounded off to 199.9mm instead of 200mm (for example). I would like the View and ViewModel layers to use millimetres instead of feet and round to the nearest 1mm, then convert this to feet in the model layer.

This applies to the bar length, the bar spacing and the minimum edge length. Add unit hints (mm) to the appropriate labels (bar length, diameter, spacing and minimum edge length).

title: Enable logging to file
description: There were some logging updates to the Common.Logging library to be able to send all logs to the central application repository. In addition to this, I would like to continue to append all logs to timestamped files in `%LOCALAPPDATA%/Northrop/logs/*` at a rate of one file per day. Please add this local logging to the default behaviour of the loggers in Common.Logging.

title: Add match clearing button for bar extents
description: In the Applications.Taipan panel, add a button next to the reinforcement bar matching that clears all extend IDs, extent values and bar IDs that would have been generated by the extent matching service. This should have the same behaviour as the bar matching button where it is cleared for the selected bars and if nothing is selected it clears all visible matches on the current view.

title: Use extents from adjacent matched bars
description:
For the Taipan plugin, where bars are linked to one another (i.e. have values provided in the Adjacent Reinforcement A and Adjacent Reinforcement C parameters) and the extent matching service is run, if extents are matched for one bar they should be propagated for all other bars.

This should be done by iterating from the first bar in the "group" to the last bar, in the direction from end A to end C. When the first match is found, all upstream bars should have this extent assigned. This extent is then assigned to all downstream bars until a new match is assigned, following which that new match should be assigned to all following bars.

This should be run after the initial bar matching occurs and bars that do not have a direct match have their extent ID references and values cleaned.

title: Improve Octopus browser performance
description:
The Octopus application has a handful of tool panels that use a native browser instance to display a Monaco editor, a PDF viewer or render Markdown with KaTeX. These are implemented as controls, and as a result when being used in a tab application there is significant latency when starting a new tab.

I want to transition this to an architecture where a warmed up pool or browser instances is kept alive in the application, and when a tool is opened the only start up required is to redirect the browser to the correct path and load the content.

This could be quite a fundamental shift away from the use of the current tab control. Consider this carefully to avoid virtualisation issues with this warm pool of browser windows. At most I expect that we will only need two browsers warmed at any time, I am OK with only keeping two and dealing with the initialisation cost if more than two are required.

title: Split lines at top and bottom of extrusion unions

Split lines at the top and bottom of extrusion unions. This should be done in the Common.Geometry library so that we can later use it in the Taipan application with reinforcement bars.

When a line crosses through an extrusion, a setting of top or bottom should be applied. If top, it should be assumed to be projected onto the top surface of the extrusion union. It should then be split at all of the top "steps" in the extrusion (which are already being detected in another method). A similar task should be performed for "bottom" lines.

title: Implement reinforcement with extension and termination logic depending on intersection types
description:
Use the split line by extrusion group behaviour that was implemented previously in the Common.Geometry library as the basis for a new command "SplitBar" in the Taipan library. This should follow the same pattern of using the abstract reinforcement bar representation to pull the Revit geometry into simplified two dimensional bars, then apply the split bar algorithm to the abstract geometry (such that it is testable) before converting back to Revit geometry and modifying the Revit model.

First, create the abstract version of this process with tests, similar to how the reinforcement and extent matching algorithm works. A bar should be designated as either "top" or "bottom" and should be selected in the Revit model. Then, the "top" or "bottom" bars should be split as appropriate by the algorithm being performed. 

This command should also handle bar extension and termination behaviour based on the type of intersection encountered (e.g. with an step or edge). Tag the ends of each segment after the split with information on the termination *type* that was encountered. This is directional - the terminations should be applied at both the start and end of a bar, with the meaning changing slightly for some of the termination types depending on whether they are at the start or end. Given that the bars that are being split are single bars, the resulting bar segments will have the same normal vector. The list of bar segments should therefore be kept in order such that the end termination of a bar in the list is at the same intersection point of the start termination of the next bar (unless clipped at a hole), although the termination types don't necessarily need to match. 

Each termination will also have a "TerminationDetail" applied, which is the physical modification at the end of the bar to suit the condition that it is in.

TerminationTypes:

- Free: if a bar starts or ends within a floor slab, the start termination of the start segment will be "free" and/or the end termination of the end segment will be "free". Nothing is done regarding the termination.
- SlabEdgeTop: when a top bar encounters the edge of a slab. The bar should have a "CogUp" (i.e. 90 degree hook downwards) at the end, with the length set by the thickness of the slab.
- SlabEdgeBottom: when a bottom bar encounters the edge of a slab. The bar should have a "CogDown" termination detail (i.e. 90 degree hook upwards) at the end, with the length of the cog set by the thickness of the slab.
- TopStepUp: A step in the top of the slab going up in the direction of the bar - i.e. the bar that *ends* with this termination is lower than the bar that *starts* with this termination. Only top reinforcement can encounter this. The bar before the termination should have the termination detail set to "FullLap", and the bar after the termination should have the termination detail set to "CogDownZ", with the length of the cog set by the total thickness of the slab.
- TopStepDown: A step in the top of the slab going down in the direction of the bar. The termination details should be applied to the opposite bars as the TopStepUp termination type.
- BottomStepUp: A step in the bottom of the slab going up in the direction of the bar - i.e. the bar that *ends* with this termination is lower than the bar that *starts* with this termination. Only bottom reinforcement can encounter this. The bar before the termination should have the termination detail set to "CogUpZ", and the bar after the termination should have the termination detail set to "FullLap", with the length of the cog set by the total thickness of the slab.
- BottomStepDown. A step in the top of the slab going down in the direction of the bar. The termination details should be applied to the opposite bars as the BottomStepUp termination type.

Note on termination types: When setting the termination details according to the termination types, the details mentioned above are "defaults" that should be applied only if there is room for the detail type to fit as these often run past the step. For example, a 12mm bar may have a 500mm lap length, so if meets a TopStepUp and has a FullLap applied but there is a slab edge just 300mm away, the bar should still continue past the step but instead of the FullLap it should have a CogDown applied as it must cog down at the slab edge. To do this, a length should be calculated for all TerminationDetails. If the length cannot be achieved in the space that exists between the intersection point and a step/edge that would block the bar, the bar should just continue and treat the step/edge as a SlabEdge* termination type.

TerminationDetails:

Each of these termination details also includes some metadata - they should be a new set of types that all implement some IBarTermination interface.

Additional end characteristics should be applied to bars given the following splitting locations:
- CogUp: A 90 degree bend up
- CogUpZ: A 90 degree bend up that ends in a flattened length equal to a "standard" cog length
- CogDown: A 90 degree bend down
-CogDownZ: A 90 degree bend down that ends in a flattened length equal to a "standard" cog length
- FullLap: A full lap extension of the bar past the end point of the segment.
- HalfLap: Half of a lap extension (not used for these TerminationTypes but will be used later.

The "standard" laps and cogs will need to be calculated based on the bar sizes and concrete details. For now, create methods that take this information in as parameters but otherwise just return 150mm for cogs and 600mm for laps.

title: Cog parallel perimeter bars inwards and add extensions
description:
When the AddPerimeterBars command is run, the BarSplittingService is run to split bars. This creates cogs at the end of every bar that terminates at an edge.

After this step is run, an additional correction step should be run (just for the creation of parallel perimeter bars) that does the following:

- For bars that have cogs at the perimeter, the cogs should be turned inwards regardless of whether they are top or bottom bars.
- For bars that have free terminations (i.e. do not terminate at an edge but stop at re-entrant corners), these should have full lap extensions applied to them.

title: Ensure all lapping offsets are inwards from the slab edge for perimeter bars
description:
When laps are created in parallel perimeter bars with the bar splitter service, the offset direction for laps should be adjusted such that it is always away from the slab edge or void edge. That is, if there are no steps the offset should alternate between 200mm and 300mm from the edge as opposed to 200mm and 100mm as can happen sometimes with the current algorithm. If steps are involved these can be treated as normal.

Consider whether this should be a separate stage or a modification to the bar splitter service.
